editorial.title: 5인 미만 사업장 760만 '티슈 노동자', 언제까지 법 밖 방치하나
editorial.body: (사설) 5인 미만 사업장 노동자는 근로기준법, 중대재해처벌법 등에서 제외돼 해고, 연차휴가, 임금, 직장 내 괴롭힘 등에서 차별받고 있다. 전체 취업자 4명 중 1명인 약 763만 명이 제대로 보호받지 못하는 현실이 시급하다. |
summary: 5인 미만 사업장 760만 노동자가 근로기준법 밖에 방치돼 있다.
해고, 연차, 임금, 괴롭힘 등 기본권에서 차별받고 있다.
전체 취업자 4명 중 1명이 법적 보호를 받지 못하는 현실이 급선무다.
editorial.pullQuote: 5인 미만 사업장 노동자는 근로기준법, 중대재해처벌법 등에서 제외돼 차별받고 있다.
editorial.stance: 영세사업장 노동자의 기본 노동권 보장을 위한 법제도 개선이 시급하다.
editorial.titleTr: Over 7.6 Million 'Tissue Workers' at Micro Businesses Left Outside Legal Protections
summaryTr: 5인 미만 사업장 760만 노동자가 근로기준법 밖에 방치돼 있다.
해고, 연차, 임금, 괴롭힘 등 기본권에서 차별받고 있다.
전체 취업자 4명 중 1명이 법적 보호를 받지 못하는 현실이 급선무다.
editorial.bodyTr: Small business workers with fewer than five employees are excluded from the Labor Standards Act and the Serious Accidents Punishment Act, facing discrimination in dismissal, annual leave, wages, and workplace harassment. The reality that approximately 7.63 million workers—one out of every four employed people—lack proper legal protection demands urgent action.

editorial.title: [사설] ‘李 공소취소’ 해줄 특검을 李가 임명, 초현실 법치 농락
editorial.body: 李 공소취소 해줄 특검을 李가 임명, 초현실 법치 농락 민주당이 30일 조작 기소 특검을 출범하고 이재명 대통령에 대한 공소 취소 권한을 부여하는 법안을 발의했다. 불법 대북 송금, 대장동 사건 등 이 대통령 관련 사건을 검찰에서 강제로 이첩받아 공소 취소 여부
editorial.pullQuote: 李 공소취소 해줄 특검을 李가 임명, 초현실 법치 농락
summary: 민주당이 이재명 대통령 공소 취소 권한을 가진 특검 출범법안을 발의했다.
이 대통령이 자신의 사건 처리 권한을 가진 특검을 직접 임명하는 구조다.
법치주의를 형식적으로 농락하는 초현실적 상황이 벌어지고 있다.
editorial.stance: 대통령이 자신의 사건 처리권을 가진 특검을 통제하는 것은 법치주의의 근본을 훼손하는 행위다.

editorials.title: [사설] 하청 노조와 ‘농어민’ 가세, 반도체 뜯어먹기 혈안
editorials.body: 하청 노조와 농어민 가세, 반도체 뜯어먹기 혈안 이재명 대통령이 영업이익 15% 성과급을 요구하며 총파업을 예고한 삼성전자 노조에 대해 자신만 살겠다고 과도한 요구나 부당한 요구를 해서 국민으로부터 지탄받게 된다면, 해당 노조뿐 아니라 다른 노동자들에게도 피해를
editorials.pullQuote: 자신만 살겠다고 과도한 요구나 부당한 요구를 해서 국민으로부터 지탄받게 된다면
summary: 삼성전자 노조가 영업이익 15% 성과급을 요구하며 총파업을 예고했다.
하청 노조와 농어민까지 가세하며 반도체 산업 흔들기에 혈안이다.
과도한 요구는 국민 지탄을 받고 전체 노동자에게 피해를 줄 수 있다.
editorials.stance: 노조의 과도한 임금 요구는 산업 전체와 근로자 모두에게 해를 끼칠 수 있다는 경고

과도한 요구는 국민 지탄을 받고 전체 노동자에게 피해를 줄 수 있다.
editorials.bodyTr:
Subcontractors' unions and farmers join forces, eyeing semiconductor profits

President Lee Jae-myung has warned that if the Samsung Electronics union, which is threatening a general strike while demanding 15% of operating profit as performance bonuses, faces public criticism for making excessive or unreasonable demands in pursuit of self-interest alone, the damage will extend not only to that union but to other workers as well.

editorials.title: [사설] “통일은 폭력적”이라는 통일부 장관
editorials.body: 통일은 폭력적이라는 통일부 장관 정동영 통일부 장관이 29일 통일이라는 이야기는 굉장히 폭력적이라고 했다. 정 장관은 취임 직후부터 통일은 어렵다는 말을 반복하고 있다. 통일부 명칭도 바꾸자고 했다. 통일부 장관이 통일이란 말을 기피한다...
summary: 정동영 통일부 장관이 통일을 '폭력적'이라고 표현했다.
취임 후 통일은 어렵다는 말을 반복하고 있다.
통일부 명칭 변경까지 제안하며 통일 용어를 회피하는 중이다.
editorials.stance: 통일부 장관의 소극적 태도와 통일 용어 회피는 국민의 통일 논의를 위축시킨다.

editorial.body: 입력 업데이트 종합 26면 제주국제공항 주차장에 수학여행을 온 학생들과 관광객들을 태우려는 전세버스들이 주차되어 있다. 뉴시스 최근 학교 현장에서 소풍·수학여행 등 체험학습이 빠르게 위축하자 이재명 대통령이 직접 나서 대책 마련을 주문했지만, 교원단체들은 대통령 인식이 잘못됐다며 반발하고 나섰다. 이 대통령은 그제 국무회의에서 “책임을 안 지려고 학생들에게 좋은 기회를 빼앗는 것”이라며 “혹시 구더기 생기지 않을까 싶어서 장독을 없애버리면 안 된다”고 말했다. 이에 대해 한국교원단체총연합회(한국교총)와 전국교직원노동조합(전교조) 등은 현장의 실상을 제대로 알지 못하는 발언이라며 교사들에게 책임을 돌리지 말라는 입장을 냈다. 대통령 발언의 본뜻도 그게 아니었겠지만, 현장 체험학습 위축의 원인을 단순히 교사들의 책임 회피로 몰아갈 수는 없는 일이다. 교원단체들이 요구하는 최우선 과제는 교사가 안심하고 체험학습을 진행할 수 있도록 법적인 보호를 제공해 달라는 것이다. 결정적 계기는 2022년 강원도 춘천에 있는 초등학교 체험학습 현장에서 발생한 학생 사망사건이었다. 당시 담임교사는 업무상 과실치사 혐의로 재판에 넘겨져 1심에 이어 2심 재판에서도 유죄를 선고받았다. 학생 안전은 매우 중요하지만, 불의의 사고가 발생할 경우 교사 개인이 형사처벌의 위험을 감수하도록 하는 것은 지나친 측면이 있다. 이 사건 이후 각급 학교에서 소풍이나 수학여행을 기피하는 현상은 통계 수치로도 드러난다. 서울 지역 초등학교 605곳 중 지난해 당일치기 현장 체험학습을 한 학교는 51%에 그쳤다. 2023년(99%)에 비하면 2년 만에 반 토막이 난 셈이다. 올해 수학여행 등 숙박형 체험학습을 계획한 서울 지역 초·중·고교는 전체의 17%로 지난해(42%)보다 크게 줄었다. 한국교총은 “사고 발생 시 모든 법적 책임을 교사 개인이 짊어져야 하는 구조가 바뀌지 않는다면 체험학습 정상화는 요원할 수밖에 없다”고 지적했다. 전교조도 “교사들이 모든 책임을 지는 구조를 바꿔야 한다”고 했다. 이 대통령이 언급한 현장 안전 인력이나 비용 지원 정도로는 현장 교사들의 불안감을 해소할 수 없다는 주장이다. 교육부도 “체험학습 중 발생한 안전사고로부터 교사를 두텁게 보호하고자 법령 개정을 추진 중”이라고 밝힌 만큼 실효적인 대책을 서두르길 바란다.
editorial.pullQuote: 사고 발생 시 모든 법적 책임을 교사 개인이 짊어져야 하는 구조가 바뀌지 않는다면 체험학습 정상화는 요원할 수밖에 없다
summary: 학교 현장 체험학습이 급격히 위축되고 있다.
2022년 학생 사망사건 이후 교사들이 형사처벌 위험을 안고 있다.
법적 보호 없이는 체험학습 정상화가 불가능하다.
editorial.stance: 교사의 책임만 강조할 것이 아니라 법적 보호 강화로 체험학습을 정상화해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: [사설] 사라져 가는 수학여행, 교사 책임만 따질 일 아니다
summaryTr: Field experience learning at schools is rapidly declining.
After a student death incident in 2022, teachers face the risk of criminal prosecution.
Normalizing experiential learning is impossible without legal protection.
editorial.bodyTr:
Input Update Comprehensive Page 26 Charter buses are parked at Jeju International Airport parking lot to pick up school trip students and tourists. Newsis

As experiential learning such as school excursions and field trips has rapidly declined in schools, President Lee Jae-myung has stepped forward to order measures to address the issue. However, teacher organizations have pushed back, saying the president's understanding is misguided.

The president said at a cabinet meeting the other day, "We are depriving students of good opportunities because we don't want to take responsibility," and "We shouldn't throw away the kimchi jar just because we're worried maggots might appear."

In response, the Korean Teachers and Educational Workers Union and the Korean Federation of Teachers' Unions issued statements that the president's remarks reflect a lack of understanding about actual conditions in schools and called for teachers not to be blamed. Though the president's original intention may not have been that, the decline in field experiential learning cannot simply be attributed to teachers avoiding responsibility.

The top priority demanded by teacher organizations is to provide legal protections so teachers can conduct experiential learning with peace of mind. The decisive catalyst was a student death incident that occurred during a field trip at an elementary school in Chuncheon, Gangwon Province in 2022. At the time, the homeroom teacher was referred to trial on charges of negligent homicide and was found guilty in both the first and second trials.

While student safety is extremely important, it is excessive to require individual teachers to bear the risk of criminal punishment when unforeseen accidents occur. Since this incident, the phenomenon of schools avoiding excursions or field trips has been evident in statistical data. Among 605 elementary schools in Seoul, only 51 percent conducted same-day field trips last year. Compared to 2023 at 99 percent, this represents a 50 percent drop in just two years. This year, only 17 percent of elementary, middle, and high schools in Seoul have planned overnight experiential learning such as field trips, down significantly from 42 percent last year.

The Korean Teachers and Educational Workers Union pointed out, "Normalization of experiential learning will remain distant unless the structure in which teachers must bear all legal responsibility in case of accidents changes." The Korean Federation of Teachers' Unions also said, "The structure in which teachers bear all responsibility must be changed."

They argue that the on-site safety personnel and financial support mentioned by the president cannot alleviate the anxiety felt by teachers in the field. The Education Ministry has announced that it is "pursuing amendments to laws and ordinances to provide robust protections for teachers from safety accidents during experiential learning," and we hope effective measures will be expedited.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 교사 보호와 법적 책임 제한을 주된 주장으로 하며 교원단체의 입장을 대폭 옹호하고, 대통령의 개인 책임 강조를 지나친 것으로 비판하는 진보적 기조를 보임.

editorial.title: Regulatory flashpoint
editorial.body: Seoul must enforce fair rules without fueling needless friction with Washington A regulatory label, obscure to most consumers, has become an unlikely fault line. On Wednesday, South Korea’s Fair Trade Commission designated Coupang chairman Kim Bom-suk, widely known as Bom Kim, as the “same person” of the group. The shift from a corporate to an individual designation, the first since 2021, aims to tighten accountability. It also reflects an effort to align formal oversight with the realities of control in complex corporate structures. Yet the decision has begun to echo beyond competition policy. The rationale is not difficult to trace. The earlier exception rested on the assumption that the founder’s relatives were not involved in management. That premise weakened after a 2025 data breach affecting more than 33 million users prompted closer scrutiny. Investigators found that Kim’s younger brother, Kim Yoo-seok, exercised influence over operations, including logistics and delivery policy, while receiving compensation well above typical executive levels. Control appeared more personal and harder to separate from the founder himself, reinforcing the case for individual designation under existing rules. Kim and his family now face expanded disclosure requirements covering overseas affiliates and related-party transactions, as well as stricter oversight of intra-group dealings. Rules governing self-dealing will apply with greater force. The decision seeks to close a gap in which control and responsibility had diverged. For a platform that helps shape local markets and supply chains, that principle carries both regulatory and economic weight. Coupang has chosen to contest the move. The company argues that its US listing already subjects it to stringent oversight by the Securities and Exchange Commission, making Korea’s designation duplicative and potentially conflicting. It has also taken its case to Washington, reportedly spending more than $1 million on lobbying and framing the issue as discrimination against US-linked technology firms. The company, however, argues that its lobbying was focused on Korea-US economic cooperation and visa issues. Some Korean lawmakers have expressed concern, warning that the case could spill into broader trade frictions. There is a domestic counterargument. Korean conglomerates such as Samsung and SK operate under the same framework without exception. Exempting a founder on the basis of foreign citizenship would invite claims of reverse discrimination and weaken enforcement credibility. Sovereignty in competition policy is not optional, particularly when a firm’s core business, workforce and market influence are overwhelmingly domestic. The difficulty lies in how quickly such disputes migrate across domains. South Korea’s national security leadership has acknowledged that the issue is complicating discussions with Washington. Media outlets here reported US concerns over Korea’s business environment alongside hesitation on sensitive defense matters. Part of the strain reflects a mismatch between regulatory frameworks and economic reality. Korea’s “same person” system, devised in the 1980s to discipline family-controlled conglomerates, sits uneasily with globally listed firms. Comparable foreign-invested companies have often retained corporate designations, raising questions about consistency and predictability. Even when justified, the framework can appear idiosyncratic to external observers and investors assessing regulatory risk. None of this invalidates the FTC’s decision. The evidence appears to meet the legal threshold, and equal application remains sound. But policy does not operate in isolation. Where enforcement intersects with geopolitics, perception can carry as much weight as principle, particularly in an alliance where economic and security interests increasingly overlap. The task is twofold: Korean authorities must enforce competition law with consistency, while explaining its rationale with greater precision to international partners through sustained engagement. At the same time, the Korean government should review whether its corporate governance framework reflects current global standards. A regulatory label should not evolve into a strategic liability, nor obscure the broader goal of transparent and accountable corporate governance. summary: South Korea's FTC designated Coupang founder Kim Bom-suk as individual liable, tightening accountability after a data breach exposed family influence.
Coupang contests the ruling, claiming US SEC oversight makes Korean designation redundant and lobbying Washington on the issue.
Seoul must enforce competition rules consistently while managing geopolitical friction with Washington over Korea's regulatory framework.
editorial.stance: Korea should enforce fair regulatory standards consistently, but must communicate policy rationale clearly to avoid needless US friction.
summaryTr: 한국 공정거래위원회가 쿠팡 창업자 김범석을 개인 책임자로 지정했으며, 데이터 유출 사건 이후 가족 영향력이 드러나면서 책임 추궁을 강화했다.
쿠팡은 미국 증권거래위원회의 감시로 인해 한국의 지정이 중복된다고 주장하며 판정에 이의를 제기하고 워싱턴에 로비 활동을 벌이고 있다.
서울은 경쟁 규칙을 일관되게 시행하면서 동시에 한국의 규제 체계를 둘러싼 워싱턴과의 지정학적 마찰을 관리해야 한다.

Control appeared more personal and harder to separate from the founder himself, reinforcing the case for individual designation under existing rules. Kim and his family now face expanded disclosure requirements covering overseas affiliates and related-party transactions, as well as stricter oversight of intra-group dealings. Rules governing self-dealing will apply with greater force. The decision seeks to close a gap in which control and responsibility had diverged. For a platform that helps shape local markets and supply chains, that principle carries both regulatory and economic weight. Coupang has chosen to contest the move. The company argues that its US listing already subjects it to stringent oversight by the Securities and Exchange Commission, making Korea’s designation duplicative and potentially conflicting. It has also taken its case to Washington, reportedly spending more than $1 million on lobbying and framing the issue as discrimination against US-linked technology firms. The company, however, argues that its lobbying was focused on Korea-US economic cooperation and visa issues. Some Korean lawmakers have expressed concern, warning that the case could spill into broader trade frictions. There is a domestic counterargument. Korean conglomerates such as Samsung and SK operate under the same framework without exception. Exempting a founder on the basis of foreign citizenship would invite claims of reverse discrimination and weaken enforcement credibility. Sovereignty in competition policy is not optional, particularly when a firm’s core business, workforce and market influence are overwhelmingly domestic. The difficulty lies in how quickly such disputes migrate across domains. South Korea’s national security leadership has acknowledged that the issue is complicating discussions with Washington. Media outlets here reported US concerns over Korea’s business environment alongside hesitation on sensitive defense matters. Part of the strain reflects a mismatch between regulatory frameworks and economic reality. Korea’s “same person” system, devised in the 1980s to discipline family-controlled conglomerates, sits uneasily with globally listed firms. Comparable foreign-invested companies have often retained corporate designations, raising questions about consistency and predictability. Even when justified, the framework can appear idiosyncratic to external observers and investors assessing regulatory risk. None of this invalidates the FTC’s decision. The evidence appears to meet the legal threshold, and equal application remains sound. But policy does not operate in isolation. Where enforcement intersects with geopolitics, perception can carry as much weight as principle, particularly in an alliance where economic and security interests increasingly overlap. The task is twofold: Korean authorities must enforce competition law with consistency, while explaining its rationale with greater precision to international partners through sustained engagement. At the same time, the Korean government should review whether its corporate governance framework reflects current global standards. A regulatory label should not evolve into a strategic liability, nor obscure the broader goal of transparent and accountable corporate governance. summaryTr: Allegations of money and valuables being exchanged in the Democratic Party primary in the Honam region continue to emerge.
Party leadership has also become embroiled in suspicions of irregularities, including the distribution of cash.
Calls are emerging for fundamental political reform.
editorial.bodyTr: The June 3 local elections are drawing near, and suspicions of wrongdoing related to primary elections—including allegations of bribery—are mounting in the Honam region. All of these incidents have emerged during the candidate selection process for the Democratic Party, which has traditionally held the region as its stronghold. In early April, Democratic Party leadership faced questions when former Jeollabuk-do Governor Kim Gwan-young distributed cash to fellow diners at a restaurant shortly after suspicions surfaced.

editorials.bodyTr: The acquittal rate in sexual crime cases handled through citizen participation trials has been found to be significantly higher than in cases processed through regular trials. This is due to the characteristics of sexual crime cases, where it is difficult for victims to properly testify about their victimization before jurors, and there is often a lack of evidence beyond their testimony. While enhancing the democratic legitimacy of the judiciary through citizen participation is important, we must also consider whether this system adequately protects victims and pursues truth in sexual assault cases. The judiciary should carefully examine whether adjustments are needed to ensure that the participation of ordinary citizens does not inadvertently create loopholes that benefit perpetrators. Sexual crime cases require special consideration given their sensitive nature and the vulnerability of victims during the trial process. Policymakers and judicial officials must work together to find a balance that upholds both democratic principles and victim protection.

청와대 정책실은 삼성전자 파업 관련 보고서를 이재명 대통령에게 올렸고, 이 대통령도 삼성전자 노조를 겨냥한 것으로 해석할 수 있는 발언을 했다.
이 대통령은 삼성전자 노조에 대한 직접적인 언급을 피하면서도 "일부 조직 노동자들이 자신들만 살겠다고 과도한 요구, 부당한 요구를 해서 국민들로부..
editorial.titleTr: 대통령까지 나서 "과도한 요구"… 삼전 노조 전향적 결정을
summaryTr: The presidential office is on high alert over Samsung Electronics' union's warning of a general strike.

The presidential office's policy team submitted a report on the Samsung Electronics strike to President Lee Jae-myung, and the president made remarks that can be interpreted as targeting the Samsung Electronics union.

While avoiding direct mention of the Samsung Electronics union, President Lee said that "some organized workers are making excessive and unreasonable demands, claiming they alone should survive, causing hardship for the people..."

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 노조의 요구를 '과도한', '부당한'으로 표현하고 대통령의 비판을 정당화하는 프레임으로, 노동자의 입장보다 경영진과 정부의 관점을 대변하고 있습니다.

editorials.titleTr: Labor Day: A Time to Think of Young People Left Without Work
summaryTr: 오늘(5월 1일)은 64년 만에 다시 맞은 노동절이다.
'근로자의 날'에서 명칭을 복원하고, 법정공휴일로 지정한 것은 노동의 가치를 바로 세우고 '쉴 권리'를 보장하겠다는 선언이라는 점에서 의미가 크다.

editorials.bodyTr: Today, May 1st, marks Labor Day celebrated again after 64 years. The restoration of the holiday's name and its designation as a legal public holiday carry great significance in that they represent a declaration to properly value labor and guarantee the "right to rest." However, it is difficult to celebrate this day as a mere festival. The number of young people pushed over an "employment cliff" without even gaining an opportunity to work is rapidly increasing. Given the current state of youth employment...

editorials.body: 도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령이 29일(현지시간) SNS를 통해 "독일에 주둔한 미군의 감축 가능성을 검토하고 있다"고 밝혔다. 복잡한 배경이 있겠지만 "미국 전체가 이란에 굴욕을 당하고 있다"고 한 프리드리히 메르츠 독일 총리의 최근 언급이 트럼프를 자극했을 가능성이 거론된다. 외교 세계에서 벌어지는 많은 일은 구조적인 요인에 우발적 요소가 더해진 결과물일 ..

editorial.title: 취약산업 '핀셋 지원'하는 EU의 高유가 대응 [사설]
editorial.body: 취약산업 '핀셋 지원'하는 EU의 高유가 대응 [사설] , 오피니언
editorial.pullQuote: EU는 취약산업에 대한 핀셋 지원으로 고유가 시대의 경제 충격을 효과적으로 관리하고 있다.
summary: EU가 고유가 위기 속 취약산업에 선별적 지원을 추진하고 있다.
핀셋 방식으로 피해 산업을 집중 지원해 경제 충격을 최소화한다.
에너지 가격 급등에 대한 유럽의 실용적 대응 전략이 주목된다.
